title: Index-set mismatch: cor:rec applied to all eta in N^n in proof of thm:main

comments.author: human
comments.body:
In the proof of `thm:main` the master expansion is summed over **all** $\eta\in\mathbb{N}^n$:
$$F^*_\nu=\sum_{\eta\in\mathbb{N}^n}F^{*\eta}_\nu F^*_{\eta^-},\qquad F^{*\eta}_\nu=\sum_\kappa a^\eta_\kappa c^\kappa_\nu,$$
and the solution then substitutes $F^*_{\eta^-}=F^*_\eta/e^*_{n-1}$ 'using that $\eta$ has a unique $0$-part.'

But `cor:rec` requires $\eta$ to (i) have **no part equal to $1$**, and (ii) have exactly **one** zero part so that the number of nonzero parts is $k=n-1$ and the deleted column has size $n-1$. Generic $\eta\in\mathbb{N}^n$ satisfy neither; for such $\eta$ the equality $F^*_{\eta^-}=F^*_\eta/e^*_{n-1}$ is simply false.

The substitution is therefore unjustified as written. To repair, one must either:
- prove the expansion is supported only on **restricted** $\eta$ (e.g. $F^{*\eta}_\nu=0$, equivalently $a^\eta_\kappa c^\kappa_\nu=0$, for non-restricted $\eta$), or
- use the correct $\eta$-dependent factor $e^*_{k(\eta)}$, which then breaks the identification with the single $P_j/e^*_{n-1}$ normalization in `prop:transition`.

Without closing this, the left-eigenvector identity $F^*_\nu=\sum_\eta F^*_\eta P(\eta,\nu)$ does not follow.
